title: monitoring — product analytics
note: This module IS the monitoring lane (Lane 1 per growth-and-observability.md). Its ANALYTICS.json declares product-analytics events that the growth::analytics lane emits about THIS MODULE's admin surfaces (health check + alert policy + provider CRUD, alert + incident management). Monitoring-lane events themselves (health check runs, fired alerts) are NOT lane-4 events — they're lane-1 signals routed to the monitoring providers, not analytics. Consent tier for system-health signals is 'essential' (cannot be opted out).

events.name: Health Check Created
events.description: Admin configured a new health check. Powers onboarding funnel + check-mix insight.

events.name: Monitoring Alert Fired
events.description: P1 fires every increment is a paging event. Consent tier: essential (system-health signal).

events.name: Monitoring Incident Escalated
events.description: P1 signal — the primary on-call missed the SLA.

events.name: Monitoring Provider Circuit Breaker Opened
events.description: P1 event — every fire indicates monitoring dispatch degradation. Consent tier: essential.

events.name: Monitoring Dashboard Viewed
events.description: Signal for engagement with the monitoring dashboard.

notes: Analytics NEVER carry raw tenant_id in provider properties — always the hashed tenant_id_bucket via PII hashing. Prevents cross-tenant correlation.
Analytics NEVER carry raw ULIDs on alerts/incidents — only their severity + signal_type + bucketed metadata.
The 'essential' consent tier for alert/incident lifecycle events is deliberate — system-health + incident-response signals cannot be opted out.

This file is DISTINCT from the monitoring-lane substrate itself. Monitoring events (health check runs, fired alerts, opened incidents) are Lane 1 primary signals that route through the MonitoringProviderManager fan-out. This analytics.json is the growth::analytics feed about the admin experience of managing the monitoring surface — a different lane, different consent, different retention.
